item.title: cmd_update silently ignores unknown options
item.intent: cmd_update must reject unknown flags — silently discarding options can make users believe a flag like --force or --dry-run took effect when it did not, potentially causing unintended updates
item.description: cmd_update() parses --dry-run, --check, --force, and --no-verify but its catch-all is '* ) shift ;;' at line 3174, silently discarding unknown flags. This is especially dangerous here: a user who mistypes '--dry-run' (e.g., '--dryrun' or '--dry_run') will get no error and the update will proceed as a live update — exactly the behavior they were trying to avoid. Fix: replace '* ) shift' with '* ) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1' to match the established pattern in cmd_clean, cmd_backlog_add, etc.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_update() and locate the option parsing loop (around line 3169-3176)
Replace the catch-all '* ) shift ;;' with '* ) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;'
Verify that valid options --dry-run, --force, --no-verify still work normally
Verify that 'aishore update --typo' now prints an error and exits non-zero
acceptanceCriteria: aishore update --unknown-flag exits with a non-zero status and prints an error message
aishore update --dry-run, --force, and --no-verify --force all still work correctly
Error message matches the format used by other commands: 'Unknown option: <flag>'
